title: Long Diagonal Spear
setup: Your knight on **g5** stings {{h7}}, your king on **f7** locks {{g7}}, {{g8}} and {{f8}} — and the bishop is one move from the **a1-h8** diagonal.
hint: Step the bishop onto the **long diagonal** — the king on **h8** has nowhere to run.
explanation:
## The Idea

A gorgeous **bishop + knight + king mate**. The bishop slides onto the long diagonal and pierces all the way to h8, while the knight and king cover every flight square.

Play {{Bb2#}}.

Why is the king stuck?

- The bishop on b2 attacks h8 directly along the **a1-h8 diagonal**
- {{g7}} — covered by your king on **f7**
- {{g8}} — covered by your king on **f7**
- {{h7}} — controlled by the knight on **g5**
- The bishop is too far for {{Kxb2}}, and nothing can interpose on the long diagonal

> **Takeaway:** The fianchetto-style **a1-h8 diagonal** (or its mirror **h1-a8**) is one of the most lethal mating lanes in chess. When your bishop reaches that diagonal and the king is in the corner with one piece covering h7 — game over.
